title: NMEs production schedule 30 and 40
wikitext:
[]: NME's production schedule (3.0 & 4.0?)             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:18 am As many of you know, I'm pretty new here. This may have been discussed in detail before, or it may not have been. I have no idea, but I've just been wondering... Anime News Service in 2007 wrote: With Newtype's scoop out in Japan this weekend the floodgates of inquiry on the 4 new Evangelion movies have apparently opened wide. While the Newtype information is new to general public, like most print magazines the material was most likely put together several weeks to a month in advance of publication. In that time new things with story have happened and Sankei Shimbun has interesting new information on the project already. First off they mention the original 3 Eva films made 4.5 billion Yen in total at the Japanese boxoffice. The manga published by Kadokawa Shoten has exceeded 15 million copies sold over the existing 10 volumes. Videos and DVD's sold exceed 4.5 million copies and about 30 billion Yen. Total goods sales exceeds 100 billion Yen. While the overall project is being called "Evangelion Shin Gekijou Ban" as mentioned in Newtype, the individual films are being titled "REBUILD OF EVANGELION". The First Volume, REBUILD OF EVANGELION:01 is due out in early summer 2007 it will have an estimated running time of 90 minutes. The Second Part, REBUILD OF EVANGELION:02 is expected in January 2008, also with a running time of 90 minutes. The Latter Part + Last Program of the series, REBUILD OF EVANGELION:03-04 are expected in early summer (possibly June) of 2008, they will have an estimated length of 45 minutes each. Additionally, ANS has learned the 3rd part (Latter Part) (which corresponds to the events of the movies and episodes 25 and 26 of the TV series) and the 4th "Last Program" will be mostly new stories and material. Parts 1-3 will use some key animation sequences from the original TV series, with new backgrounds (settings) and music. Shinji Higuchi is confirmed on the project and has already completed a new story board for the 1st film. A good deal of 3DCG animation will be implemented. The total economic effect of the 4 new Evangelion films is estimated at around 100 billion Yen or more. Director Hideaki Anno has chosen this point in time specifically to produce the new Eva, waiting until the hype of the previous works to cooled down.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:38 am Probably both. The schedule never seemed all that realistic, especially after 1.0 already saw a delay from "early summer" to September (which is hardly "early"). Add a lot of backtracking to redo things in 1.0 and who knows how many changes the rest of the movies saw from the original plans of maybe really just being supposed to be a flashy version of the old animation with minor differences to the original series, and we're pretty much were we are now.

[]:             Quote Post by Desty_Nova » Wed Jul 08, 2009 4:41 am I hope this drastically altered schedule means 3.0 and 4.0 will be full length features.

[]:             Quote Post by Lagann » Wed Jul 08, 2009 6:32 am I think they drasticlly altered what they had planned to do right after 1.0,hence the delay.I think an example of that is when they went back and put halos over all the angels it was because they originally planed to do just a animation remake but SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by The Eva Monkey » Wed Jul 08, 2009 10:29 am Working in creative myself, I can say that schedules are a vague and amorphous concept that only really exist to kick you in the butt to work as hard as possible to get it done with as little time past the "deadline" as possible. Deadlines are like rainbows and unicorns. They only exist in certain circumstances.

[]:             Quote Post by JimmyWolk » Wed Jul 08, 2009 10:34 am The Eva Monkey wrote: They only exist in certain circumstances.

[]:             Quote Post by Evafan » Wed Jul 08, 2009 10:49 am Lagann wrote: I think they drasticlly altered what they had planned to do right after 1.0,hence the delay.

[]:             Quote Post by Electric Sachiel » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:09 am Evafan wrote: Lagann wrote: I think they drasticlly altered what they had planned to do right after 1.0,hence the delay.

[]:             Quote Post by I Accidentally Dracula » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:32 am JimmyWolk wrote: especially after 1.0 already saw a delay from "early summer" to September (which is hardly "early").

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Wed Jul 08, 2009 11:45 am That is most certainly true. Look at Legend of Zelda Twilight Princess. That was originally intended to come out on the Gamecube, while the Wii didn't even exist yet. Constant delays pushed it so far back that the Wii was out, and they ended up releasing on both, and even STILL the game wasn't quite as good as people were hoping.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 08, 2009 12:15 pm Electric Sachiel wrote: I just hope we don't have to wait another three years for the next two to come out. Frankly granted it takes time to animate these things but do hope the wait won't be longer than 3 years.

[]:             Quote Post by Axell » Wed Jul 08, 2009 12:20 pm i always suspected that. otherwise they could use 2010 as its 15 years after the series. meh

[]:             Quote Post by Sammaeloo » Wed Jul 08, 2009 12:48 pm Maybe he could use the hatemail in the mind tripping Instrumentality scenes like he did with EoE.

[]:             Quote Post by i_Love_Asuka » Wed Jul 08, 2009 12:49 pm Reichu wrote: They'll find some way to drag out the release schedule to 2015, at which point the new ending in the fourth movie will trigger the apocalypse during its first theatrical run.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:13 pm i_Love_Asuka wrote: that i MUST pre-order the tickets for that show because their will be just one theatrical run EVER!!!

[]:             Quote Post by Jinroh » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:15 pm Agentomega wrote: That is most certainly true. Look at Legend of Zelda Twilight Princess. That was originally intended to come out on the Gamecube, while the Wii didn't even exist yet. Constant delays pushed it so far back that the Wii was out, and they ended up releasing on both, and even STILL the game wasn't quite as good as people were hoping.

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:29 pm Reichu wrote: Electric Sachiel wrote: I just hope we don't have to wait another three years for the next two to come out. Frankly granted it takes time to animate these things but do hope the wait won't be longer than 3 years.

[]:             Quote Post by BattleMonkey » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:38 pm With how successful these films seem to be, I would think that they would want to try to extend this as much as possible and make 3 and 4 completely separate films.

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Jul 08, 2009 1:43 pm @Agentomega: This will necessitate a group trip to Japan. We'll be like an apocalyptic cult and all die together. Tongue-in-cheek aside, I wouldn't be too astonished if we had to wait that long. Being sidetracked by revisionalism helps, but ultimately depends on whether or not parts 3 and 4 are still going to be back-and-back a la EoE, or whether they've been inflated into two entirely separate films. I'm honestly hoping that Anno has enough "lolwtf" insanity up his sleeves to fill two whole more movies with stuff we can't possibly anticipate. SPOILER: Show

[]:             Quote Post by Agentomega » Wed Jul 08, 2009 2:57 pm Reichu wrote: @Agentomega: This will necessitate a group trip to Japan. We'll be like an apocalyptic cult and all die together. Tongue-in-cheek aside, I wouldn't be too astonished if we had to wait that long. Being sidetracked by revisionalism helps, but ultimately depends on whether or not parts 3 and 4 are still going to be back-and-back a la EoE, or whether they've been inflated into two entirely separate films. I'm honestly hoping that Anno has enough "lolwtf" insanity up his sleeves to fill two whole movies with stuff we can't possibly anticipate. SPOILER: Show
